title: angelica acutiloba
intro: Angelica acutiloba, also known as Japanese angelica, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its large, fern-like leaves and tall, branching stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating menstrual cramps, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory effects. Angelica acutiloba is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese kampo.
